articles.title: Centre clamps down on Telegram again, demands 'anti-piracy' action within 15 days
articles.summary: The Centre issued a notice to Telegram, asking it to take action on pirated content within 15 days.
articles.content:
Telegram has come again under Centre's radar over “widespread dissemination of pirated films, OTT content and other audio-visual material”. Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (I&B) issued a notice to the messaging platform, asking it to take anti-piracy action within 15 days. The messaging platform

Directing Telegram to tighten anti-piracy measures, the government has sets 15-day deadline “to strengthen systems for detection, reporting, disabling access to and removal of pirated films and infringing audio-visual content.” The Ministry also instructed the messaging platform to act against infringers, including channels, groups, bots, accounts, administrators and associated entities.

“The communication signals a clear shift from piecemeal takedown to platform accountability. The Government earlier acted against over 3,000 Telegram channels carrying pirated content,” the government in a statement said. Seeking information on anti-piracy measures, it noted that it is mandatory for the platform to comply with the requirements as per IT Act and IT Rules.

Centre further warned that if Telegram failed to comply to “prevent, detect and remove pirated content from the platform” and to submit an Action Taken Report then it could attract legal action under the Copyright Act, 1957 and Cinematograph Act, 1952.

Noting that the government earlier flagged over 3,000 Telegram channels carrying pirated content, the Ministry sought details of Telegram’s grievance redressal system for producers, OTT platforms, and law-enforcement agencies. Calling for a shift from piecemeal takedown to platform accountability, the Centre clarified that a purely reactive, “channel-by-channel takedown approach may not be enough to show due diligence.”

articles.title: Yes Bank Q1 business update is out; shares to be in focus on Monday: Check details
articles.summary: Yes Bank shares declined 2% in a week but gained 6% in a month and 13% in 2026, closing at ₹24.39. CASA deposits rose 14.3% YoY, while the bank's board approved a proposal to raise ₹16,000 crore through equity and debt issuances, pending approvals.
articles.content:
Yes Bank Q1 Business Update: Yes Bank share price will be in focus on Monday, July 6, after the private sector lender reported loans and advances of ₹2.85 lakh crore for the April-June quarter of FY27, marking an 18.4% year-on-year (YoY) rise from ₹2.41 lakh crore reported in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year.

Sequentially, loans and advances rose 4% from ₹2.73 lakh crore reported in the fourth quarter of FY26. The private lender's deposits meanwhile increased more than 14% YoY to ₹3.15 lakh crore during Q1 FY27 from ₹2.76 lakh crore in Q1 FY26. However, deposits declined more than 1% quarter-on-quarter (QoQ) from ₹3.19 lakh crore reported in Q4 FY26.

Yes Bank's current account savings account (CASA) deposits rose 14.3% YoY to ₹1.03 lakh crore during the first quarter of FY27 but declined around 8% QoQ. The bank's CASA deposits stood at ₹1.03 lakh crore, up 14.3% YoY but down 7.8% QoQ from ₹1.12 lakh crore. The CASA ratio, including certificates of deposit (CDs), stood at 32.7%, compared with 35.1% in the previous quarter and 32.8% a year earlier.

Certificates of deposit declined to ₹6,604 crore from ₹6,831 crore in the preceding quarter. The credit-to-deposit ratio improved to 90.5% as of June 30, 2026, from 85.7% at the end of March and 87.4% a year earlier.

The bank's average quarterly Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) on a consolidated basis stood at 138.5%, compared with 119.0% in the previous quarter and 135.8% in the corresponding quarter last year. The credit-to-deposit ratio meanwhile rose to 90.5%.

Yes Bank said these figures are provisional and have been released ahead of the announcement of its financial results for the quarter ended June 30, 2026. It added that the actual financial earnings will be announced after the audit.

This week, Yes Bank's board approved a proposal to raise up to ₹16,000 crore through a combination of equity and debt issuances. The board cleared an enabling resolution to raise up to ₹7,500 crore through eligible equity securities and up to ₹8,500 crore through eligible debt securities in one or more tranches, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

The bank said the equity fundraising, together with any dilution arising from the conversion of eligible convertible debt securities, will not result in an aggregate dilution of more than 10% of the existing share capital.

The proposed fundraising may be undertaken through various permissible routes in domestic and overseas markets. The debt issuance may also include instruments denominated in Indian or foreign currencies. The proposals will be placed before shareholders for approval at the bank's 22nd Annual General Meeting, scheduled to be held on August 19.

Yes Bank shares have fallen around 2% in one week but gained more than 6% in one month and 13% in 2026 so far to close at ₹24.39 apiece on Friday. Over the longer term, the stock has surged 21% in one year, 49% in three years and 80% in five years.

After hitting a 52-week low of ₹17.20 apiece in March, Yes Bank shares rallied 50% in less than three months to touch a 52-week high of ₹25.78 apiece in June. The stock has since declined more than 5% from that level.

articles.title: Unity Software (U) Stock Loses Its Place In Major Russell Growth Indexes
articles.summary: Unity Software (U) has been removed from several major Russell growth indexes, including the Russell 1000 Growth and 3000 Growth benchmarks. This exclusion may impact the stock's visibility among institutional investors and index-tracking funds, leading to potential selling pr...
articles.content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

  • Unity Software (NYSE:U) has been removed as a constituent from several major Russell growth indexes.

  • The changes affect the Russell 1000 Growth, 2500 Growth, 3000 Growth, 3000E Growth, Small Cap Comp Growth, and Midcap Growth benchmarks.

  • This index exclusion may influence Unity Software's visibility with institutional investors and index-tracking funds.

Unity Software now sits outside some of the largest growth benchmarks while its stock trades around $29.32. The shares are down 33.7% year to date, up 9.8% over the past week, and up 12.2% over the past year, which highlights the volatility in sentiment around NYSE:U. Over a 3 year and 5 year span, the stock has declined 27.7% and 72.1%, placing the index removal in the context of a longer period of pressure.

For investors, the key question is how this change in index status might intersect with Unity Software's future role in game development and real time 3D tools. Index reshuffles can affect flows for a period, and your focus may be on whether the business metrics and the balance between growth and profitability eventually support a market profile that differs from the one implied by these benchmark exits.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Unity Software by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Unity Software.

The removal of Unity Software from several Russell growth indexes is a clear signal about how the stock currently screens on size and growth-style criteria, rather than a direct judgment on its technology or customer base. Index providers periodically rebalance based on rules like market capitalization, liquidity, and style factors. When a company falls out of multiple benchmarks at once, index-tracking funds that mirror those benchmarks typically sell, which can add mechanical selling pressure around the effective date and reduce passive ownership over time. For you as an investor, this shifts the mix of who might own Unity Software, tilting it more toward active managers and individual investors who are making a deliberate call on the business. At the same time, separate commentary shows analysts maintaining generally positive views on Unity Software's earnings outlook and rating, so the index changes sit alongside, rather than replace, those fundamental opinions.

articles.title: Deckers Outdoor highlights resilient branded footwear demand
articles.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corp. maintains strong demand for its footwear brands by focusing on premium positioning and omnichannel distribution, despite a mixed consumer environment. The company's strategy involves brand-led growth, emphasizing profitability, disciplined expansion, and...
articles.content:
Deckers Outdoor Corp. (ISIN US2441991054) is a global footwear and apparel company known for its portfolio of lifestyle and performance brands and its listing on a major US stock exchange. The group continues to emphasize brand strength, controlled distribution, and product innovation as central pillars of its strategy. For investors, the balance between growth spending and margin discipline remains a key theme in the stock's longer-term narrative.

Brand-led growth strategy

Deckers Outdoor Corp. has built its business around a set of distinct brands that target different segments of the footwear and apparel market. The company focuses on creating recognizable franchises within each brand and supporting them with marketing, design investment, and close control of wholesale and direct-to-consumer channels. This brand-centric approach aims to keep pricing power intact while avoiding overexposure to discount-driven volumes.

Management has historically described a strategy that favors sustainable growth over short-term volume spikes. That approach typically involves limiting distribution to chosen partners, investing in digital capabilities, and maintaining tight control over inventory to reduce markdown risk. For the stock, these decisions influence both revenue growth potential and the resilience of operating margins over the cycle.

Focus on profitability and disciplined expansion

In recent years, Deckers Outdoor Corp. has devoted considerable attention to profitability, seeking operating efficiencies in sourcing, logistics, and corporate overhead. This discipline is particularly important in the footwear and apparel sector, where input-cost swings and promotional intensity can quickly compress margins. By prioritizing higher-margin products and channels, the company aims to defend returns on invested capital even as it invests in new regions and product categories.

Deckers Outdoor Corp. also continues to develop its omnichannel capabilities. E-commerce and company-operated retail stores complement wholesale relationships, allowing the group to present its brands consistently while gathering direct consumer data. That data helps inform product design, distribution decisions, and marketing campaigns, and can support more efficient inventory planning. For investors, an effective omnichannel model can help smooth earnings volatility across seasons and regions.

Key footwear and apparel portfolios

Deckers Outdoor Corp. is best known for premium casual and performance footwear brands that have become fixtures in both fashion and outdoor markets. Within its portfolio, individual franchises often span multiple product types, from boots and sandals to running and hiking shoes, as well as associated apparel and accessories. The company typically refreshes its collections seasonally while maintaining core silhouettes that anchor consumer recognition.

The product strategy blends lifestyle appeal with functional features such as comfort, durability, and weather resistance. This combination aims to broaden the customer base beyond strictly technical users, tapping into everyday wear while retaining credibility among performance-focused buyers. Over time, such positioning can help support repeat purchases and cross-brand adoption within the same household.

Deckers Outdoor Corp. stock and valuation context

Deckers Outdoor Corp. stock represents exposure to branded footwear and apparel demand, particularly in North American and selected international markets. The market generally evaluates the company on metrics such as revenue growth, gross and operating margin trends, cash generation, and capital allocation, including share repurchases and investment in new initiatives. Valuation often reflects a balance between the perceived durability of its core brands and broader consumer-cyclical risks.

While individual trading-day price moves can be influenced by broader indices and sector sentiment, longer-term performance tends to track the company’s ability to grow its leading brands, expand direct-to-consumer channels, and maintain disciplined cost control. For many investors, the durability of brand equity and the resilience of free cash flow are central considerations in assessing Deckers Outdoor Corp. stock.
